title: Sonic X: The Metarex Saga
titleNative: ソニックX 第2期
synopsis: Sonic activates Chaos Control in his battle against Dark Oak, most formidable enemy he's faced thus far, to scatter the Chaos Emeralds across the galaxy. Meanwhile, Tails, Amy, and Cream encounter a spaceship that's crash-landed to their planet during a meteor shower. Note: The listed airing dates are from the original US broadcast. Due to poor ratings in Japan, the series cancelled airing after the first 52 episodes, and only received its first local airing in 2020.

title: Ling Long Middle Chapter
titleNative: 灵笼·中章
synopsis: In the not-too-distant future, the human world has long been overpopulated. Humanity marched to the stars in search of a new home, and just as this was going on, the moon's alignment caused massive earthquakes to ripple across the world, lasting several decades. Humanity was demolished. When the land finally recovered, humanity reemerged to face this strange yet familiar world. Will we, who once lorded over this domain, still remain the masters of this world? (Source: BiliBili)

title: Choujigen Game Neptune THE ANIMATION: Nepu no Natsuyasumi
titleRomaji: Choujigen Game Neptune THE ANIMATION: Nepu no Natsuyasumi
titleNative: 超次元ゲイム ネプテューヌ THE ANIMATION ~ねぷのなつやすみ~
synopsis: The first installment of the Neptune OVA Series Trilogy. Neptune and her friends visit "Watashi no Natsuyasumi Yama" (My Summer Vacation Mountain) filled with nature to enjoy their summer vacation. The four goddesses, who gather after a long time, are having a good time as usual. During the vacation, Nepgear and other sisters go to the mountains to wander around. After going through the stretch of a long torii (Shinto shrine archway), the sisters wander off into a different dimension. Then Nepgear meets a woman who just looks like her sister Neptune in her adult form. (Source: Crunchyroll)

title: Ms. vampire who lives in my neighborhood.
titleEnglish: Ms. vampire who lives in my neighborhood.
titleNative: となりの吸血鬼さん
synopsis: Human girl Amano Akari is rescued by a vampire, Sophie Twilight, and falls in love with her. She forces herself into Sophie’s home and begins living with her. Though Sophie is a vampire, she never attacks humans, instead buying blood and anime merchandise online like any ordinary person. A modern-day vampire comedy! (Source: Crunchyroll)

title: Laughing Under the Clouds: Gaiden Part 3
titleRomaji: Donten ni Warau Gaiden: Ouka, Tenbou no Kakehashi
titleEnglish: Laughing Under the Clouds: Gaiden Part 3
titleNative: 曇天に笑う＜外伝＞ ～桜華、天望の架橋～
synonyms: Donten: Laughing Under The Clouds - Gaiden: Chapter 3 - Conspiracy of the Military
synopsis: The third part in an anime film trilogy adaptation of the Donten ni Warau Gaiden spinoff manga. One day, Soramaru is attacked by a mysterious man with large snake scales. The mysterious man somewhat resembles Tenka and demands to know where Tenka is. Soramaru later finds that this man was the result of an experiment using cells of the Orochi. While Soramaru and members of the Yamainu search for answers, they will uncover many secrets, and fight old friends along the way in search of the truth. (Source: Eleven Arts)

title: Three Leaves, Three Colors
titleNative: 三者三葉
synopsis: Futaba Odagiri is the energetic transfer student who befriends Teru Hayama, the class president who is meaner than she looks, and Youko Nishikawa, a former rich girl who still acts like a princess. Three very different girls who only share one thing: the kanji for leaf in their names. Balancing school and life is hard enough, but what about dealing with clashing personalities on top of it all? (Source: Crunchyroll)

title: Wake Up, Girls! The Movie
titleRomaji: Wake Up, Girls! Shichinin no Idol
titleNative: Wake Up, Girls! 七人のアイドル
synopsis: Green Leaves Entertainment is in the worst situation any Talent Management Agency can be in... they have NO talent! Desperate for an act, President Junko Tange targets the idol singer market and gives her flunky Matsuda his marching orders: get a girl group to manage, even if it means building one from scratch! Since complete singing groups rarely appear out of thin air, Matsuda's now in the difficult position of having to find girls with the right skills who don't already have contracts with Japan's music industry. That girl at the Maid Café? Good enough! A lead singer who's been fired from another group? Sure, why not? How about one who's only 13 years old? What could possibly go wrong there? (Source: Sentai Filmworks)

title: Beyblade: Metal Masters
titleNative: メタルファイト ベイブレード～爆～
synopsis: After defeating Ryuga and his dark Bey L-Drago, Gingka is crowned the winner of the Battle Bladers Championship. When news gets out, other bladers from around the world want their chance at defeating Gingka and capturing the title of number one Beyblader. The WBBA, now headed by Ryo, calls a global competition enlisting top representatives from each country to compete. (Source: Nelvana)

title: Mobile Suit Gundam 00 the Movie: A Wakening of the Trailblazer
titleRomaji: Kidou Senshi Gundam 00: A wakening of the Trailblazer
titleEnglish: Mobile Suit Gundam 00 the Movie: A Wakening of the Trailblazer
titleNative: 劇場版 機動戦士ガンダム00 -A wakening of the Trailblazer-
synopsis: The year is 2314 AD, two years after Celestial Being's last great battle and the world faces a new crisis. A derelict Jupiter exploration ship, abandoned 130 years ago, has left its orbit and is approaching Earth. The ESF has also begun to exploit the power of Innovators through Descartes Shaman. The world's exposure to GN Particles has resulted in many people awakening as True Innovators. Realizing the military benefits of such individuals, the Earth Sphere Federation has begun to research Innovation and exploit the emerging Innovators' abilities. As Celestial Being and its Gundam Meisters begin their final mission to save humanity from an unimaginable threat, the Extraterrestrial Living-metal Shape-shifters (ELS), Gundam Meister Setsuna F. Seiei is about to discover the true purpose of his evolution as an Innovator and the nature of the "dialogues" for which Aeolia Schenberg's plan had prepared the human race. (Source: Wikipedia)

title: Dororo
titleNative: どろろと百鬼丸
synopsis: Hyakkimaru is a young man who lacks 48 body parts because they were taken from him by demons before birth, as payment by his father, Kagemitsu Daigo, to obtain his wish to take over the country. When the baby boy was born he was missing 48 parts of his body, and thus was abandoned—thrown into a river. Hyakkimaru has grown up and now has obtained fake body parts so he can eliminate the 48 demons that were made from his body, and to retrieve his missing parts. Along for the adventure is the boy thief, Dororo, with whom he becomes friends. (Source: Anime News Network)

title: Kyoro-chan
titleNative: キョロちゃん
synopsis: While exploring ruins on Angel Island, a doctor finds a myserious newborn bird called Kyoro-chan. Together they form a bond and decide to travel the world together. Three years later when the Doctor and Kyoro-chan try to return to Angel Island they become shipwrecked from a storm. Kyoro-chan was able to make it safely to the island but the Doctor was nowhere to be found. Kyoro-chan prays for his safety and hope he returns as he now starts his new life on Angel Island.

title: Transformers Generation 1
titleNative: 戦え!超ロボット生命体トランスフォーマー
synopsis: Fleeing from the war that has raged on the planet Cybertron for millions of years, the heroic Autobots led by Optimus Prime encounter a surprise attack by the evil Decepticons led by Megatron. The ensuing melee aboard the Autobot ship ends when the ship crashes on a volcano. Four million years later, an earthquake reactivates the ship`s computer and resurrects both the Autobots and Decepticons, resuming the great war of the Transformers. (Source: AniDB)

title: La Corda D'Oro: Primo Passo
titleNative: 金色のコルダ ～primo passo～
synopsis: Hino Kahoko is a student whose school is divided into two branches - the Normal Branch, in which students wear grey uniforms, and the Music Branch, in which the students wear white. The school's tradition is a music competition, but it it more common that students from the Music Branch are chosen to compete. Kahoko is surprised one day to encounter a fairy named Lili. Solely because she is the only student to see him, she is the only student from the Normal Branch to be in the competition. Kahoko doesn't have any musical knowledge, until Lili grants her a magic violin, one that anyone can play if they believe they can truly do it. Kahoko must now face the trials of the competition, her competitors, and her peers.

titleNative: パンプキン・シザーズ
synopsis: Alice Malvin commands a military propaganda unit, but she idealistically yearns to cut through the deception. When she uncovers a super-soldier program known as The Invisible Nine, she finds herself trapped in a web of deadly secrets. Malvin turns to an unlikely ally—Randel Orland, a hulking veteran who may hold the key to unlocking the mysteries of The Invisible Nine. (Source: Crunchyroll)

tags.description: Centers around politics, politicians, or government activities.

tags.name: Tomboy
tags.description: Features a girl who exhibits characteristics or behaviors considered in many cultures to be typical of boys.

tags.name: Tragedy
tags.description: Centers around tragic events and unhappy endings.

tags.name: Conspiracy
tags.description: Contains one or more factions controlling or attempting to control the world from the shadows.

tags.name: Guns
tags.description: Prominently features the use of guns in combat.

tags.name: Episodic
tags.description: Features story arcs that are loosely tied or lack an overarching plot.

tags.name: Shounen
tags.description: Target demographic is teenage and young adult males.

tags.name: Primarily Adult Cast
tags.description: Main cast is mostly composed of characters above a high school age.

tags.name: Foreign
tags.description: Partly or completely set in a country outside the country of origin.

tags.name: Male Protagonist
tags.description: Main character is male.

tags.name: Ensemble Cast
tags.description: Features a large cast of characters with (almost) equal screen time and importance to the plot.

tags.name: Philosophy
tags.description: Relating or devoted to the study of the fundamental nature of knowledge, reality, and existence.

tags.name: Henshin
tags.description: Prominently features character or costume transformations which often grant special abilities.

tags.name: War
tags.description: Partly or completely set during wartime.

tags.name: CGI
tags.description: Prominently features scenes created with computer-generated imagery.

tags.name: Heterosexual
tags.description: Prominently features a romance between a man and a woman, not inherently sexual.

tags.name: Unrequited Love
tags.description: One or more characters are experiencing an unrequited love that may or may not be reciprocated.

tags.name: Gore
tags.description: Prominently features graphic bloodshed and violence.
